tweets.title:
Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
7th Jun 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

●  The IDF eliminated Sakr Abu Karim, a Hamas Nukhba cell commander.

● The IDF issued an urgent evacuation warning for residents of Tyre and nearby camps, citing repeated Hezbollah ceasefire violations.

● The US shot down two Iranian drones after they allegedly threatened international maritime traffic.

● Qatar issued a NOTAM rerouting flights through its airspace, providing alternate routes for aircraft departing Doha and Saudi airports amid ongoing regional tensions.

● The IRGC Ground Force’s Quds HQ, in a joint operation with intelligence forces, dismantled a terrorist cell attempting to infiltrate the country in the Saravan area.

● OPEC+ is set to approve a fourth consecutive monthly increase in oil output targets, despite the ongoing US-Iran war disrupting production for several member countries.

● As of June 7, CENTCOM has redirected 132 commercial vessels and disabled 6 to ensure compliance with the naval blockade.

END OF THE BRIEF
tweets.content:
Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
7th Jun 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● The IDF eliminated Sakr Abu Karim, a Hamas Nukhba cell commander.

● The IDF issued an urgent evacuation warning for residents of Tyre and nearby camps, citing repeated Hezbollah ceasefire violations.

● The US shot down two Iranian drones after they allegedly threatened international maritime traffic.

● Qatar issued a NOTAM rerouting flights through its airspace, providing alternate routes for aircraft departing Doha and Saudi airports amid ongoing regional tensions.

● The IRGC Ground Force’s Quds HQ, in a joint operation with intelligence forces, dismantled a terrorist cell attempting to infiltrate the country in the Saravan area.

● OPEC+ is set to approve a fourth consecutive monthly increase in oil output targets, despite the ongoing US-Iran war disrupting production for several member countries.

● As of June 7, CENTCOM has redirected 132 commercial vessels and disabled 6 to ensure compliance with the naval blockade.

END OF THE BRIEF

Netram Defence Review (@NetramDefence)

Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
6th Jun 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● Iran launched missile strikes targeting Ali Al Salem Air Base in Kuwait and key facilities of the US Navy’s Fifth Fleet in Bahrain.

● CENTCOM forces shot down 4 Iranian one-way attack drones heading toward the Strait of Hormuz. US forces subsequently struck Iranian coastal surveillance radar sites in Goruk and on Qeshm Island.

● Bahrain’s air defences intercepted and destroyed 3 missiles and several drones launched from Iran.

● Kuwait’s armed forces detected and intercepted 7 hostile ballistic missiles in its airspace.

● The US State Department approved a military equipment sale to Kuwait that includes counter-unmanned aerial vehicle (C-UAV) systems.

● The Chinese-owned oil tanker JV INNOVATION, bulk carrier JIN YUAN MEN, and Singapore-registered oil tanker ALORA successfully crossed the Strait of Hormuz.

END OF THE BRIEF

— https://nitter.net/NetramDefence/status/2063315723498446881#m
tweets.author: @NetramDefence

tweets.title:
RT by @OsintTV: Brutal crackdown by Pakistan in PoK ahead of the June 9 shutdown.

Pakistani Army, Rangers and Punjab Police have opened fire on Awami Action Committee protesters, leaving 4 people seriously injured.

Internet services remain suspended, AAC offices have been sealed, and the group has been designated a terrorist organization.

Just a day earlier, AAC member Umar Nazir was seriously injured after Pakistani security forces allegedly opened fire on his vehicle, killing one of his associates.

The developments have revived memories of last year’s deadly crackdown in which 28 protesters were killed.

PoK is on edge.

tweets.title:
As a kid I read a chacha Chaudhry story 
It said :
A thief was caught and given a choice:
Either eat 100 onions or receive 100 shoe-beatings (sau jute).

Thinking onions are easier, he starts eating them. After a few onions, the burning becomes unbearable, so he switches to shoe-beatings.

After a few shoe-beatings, he finds those even worse and switches back to onions.

He keeps changing his mind until, in the end, he has eaten all 100 onions and received all 100 shoe-beatings.

Moral : Failure to commit to a difficult choice makes things worse.
tweets.content:
As a kid I read a chacha Chaudhry story
It said :
A thief was caught and given a choice:
Either eat 100 onions or receive 100 shoe-beatings (sau jute).

Thinking onions are easier, he starts eating them. After a few onions, the burning becomes unbearable, so he switches to shoe-beatings.

After a few shoe-beatings, he finds those even worse and switches back to onions.

He keeps changing his mind until, in the end, he has eaten all 100 onions and received all 100 shoe-beatings.

Moral : Failure to commit to a difficult choice makes things worse.

IAF to Airlift NEET Question Papers for First Time

The Indian Air Force (IAF) will be used to transport NEET-UG 2026 question papers, according to NTA Director General Abhishek Singh.
It is first-ever use of the IAF for NEET paper transportation

Authorities have also directed police to maintain strict vigilance and actively counter misinformation on social media.

tweets.title:
BREAKING: China Launches ‘Special Operation’ East of Taiwan

China has conducted a “special maritime law enforcement operation” in waters east of Taiwan, according to state media.

Beijing says the move was a response to Japan and the Philippines beginning talks to delimit a maritime boundary in the area, a process China has denounced as “illegal.”

tweets.title:
Over the last few days, I’ve met founders of several defence startups. One theme came up repeatedly: “Failure is a step in the right direction. The more we fail, the better we get.”

Yet many of India’s major defence programmes have operated in an ecosystem where the appetite for failure is extremely low. Kaveri is perhaps the most cited example.
tweets.content:
Over the last few days, I’ve met founders of several defence startups. One theme came up repeatedly: “Failure is a step in the right direction. The more we fail, the better we get.”

Yet many of India’s major defence programmes have operated in an ecosystem where the appetite for failure is extremely low. Kaveri is perhaps the most cited example.

tweets.title:
Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
6th Jun 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● Iran launched missile strikes targeting Ali Al Salem Air Base in Kuwait and key facilities of the US Navy’s Fifth Fleet in Bahrain.

● CENTCOM forces shot down 4 Iranian one-way attack drones heading toward the Strait of Hormuz. US forces subsequently struck Iranian coastal surveillance radar sites in Goruk and on Qeshm Island.

● Bahrain’s air defences intercepted and destroyed 3 missiles and several drones launched from Iran.

● Kuwait’s armed forces detected and intercepted 7 hostile ballistic missiles in its airspace.

● The US State Department approved a military equipment sale to Kuwait that includes counter-unmanned aerial vehicle (C-UAV) systems.

● The Chinese-owned oil tanker JV INNOVATION, bulk carrier JIN YUAN MEN, and Singapore-registered oil tanker ALORA successfully crossed the Strait of Hormuz.

END OF THE BRIEF

5th Jun 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● The IDF eliminated senior members of Hamas’ General Security Apparatus, as well as Abed Harb, commander of Hezbollah’s Engineering Unit.

● The Israeli Air Force conducted airstrikes on multiple towns in southern Lebanon, including a strike on the town of Kafra in the Bint Jbeil district.

● US and Israel have begun formal negotiations on a new security framework to replace the current MOU expiring in 2028, shifting toward a reciprocal defense partnership with deeper joint military development.

● A drone struck the oil export terminal at Oman’s Mina Al-Fahal port, causing an explosion. Omani authorities stated operations continue as normal.

● As of today, CENTCOM has redirected 129 commercial vessels and disabled 6 to ensure compliance with the naval blockade.

END OF THE BRIEF

tweets.title:
RT by @OsintTV: पुणे के धार्मिक मेले में बिक रही थी ‘Made In Pakistan’ बेडशीट? वायरल वीडियो के बाद मचा हड़कंप, मंदिर मेले से खरीदे गए सामान पर उठे सवाल, पुलिस जांच में जुटी, पाकिस्तान निर्मित उत्पादों की बिक्री को लेकर बढ़ी चिंता

A viral video from Pune, Maharashtra has triggered a major controversy after a woman claimed she discovered a "Made in Pakistan" label on a bedsheet purchased from a religious fair held near the historic Moraya Gosavi Temple during the Sankashti Chaturthi celebrations. The woman alleged that she found the label while washing the bedsheet at home, raising concerns over how a Pakistan-manufactured product allegedly reached a local religious marketplace in India.

Following the video's widespread circulation on social media, Pimpri-Chinchwad Police launched an investigation and formed a special team to verify the claims. Authorities have also sought action against vendors if any violation of import regulations is found. The incident comes amid heightened scrutiny over Pakistani-origin goods after India imposed restrictions on direct and indirect imports from Pakistan following the Pahalgam terror attack.

The episode has reignited debates over supply chains, product labeling, market surveillance, and the need for stricter checks on goods being sold at public fairs and religious gatherings across the country.

#Pune #Maharashtra #MadeInPakistan #ViralVideo #PimpriChinchwad MorayaGosaviTemple #SankashtiChaturthi IndiaNews BreakingNews PoliceInvestigation ReligiousFair PakistanProducts ViralStory TrendingNews IndianMarkets SecurityConcerns NewsUpdate #PublicAlert #SouthAsiaNews #CurrentAffairs
tweets.content:

State-owned Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders Ltd is emerging as the lead anchor investor for a massive ₹29,253 crore mega shipbuilding cluster in Dugarajapatnam, Andhra Pradesh.

The ambitious maritime manufacturing hub will span approximately 3,500 acres and target 1.2 million gross tonnage of annual shipbuilding capacity.

tweets.title:
RT by @OsintTV: ऑपरेशन ब्लू स्टार की 42वीं बरसी पर अमृतसर में फिर चर्चा के केंद्र में भिंडरांवाले, श्री अकाल तख्त साहिब परिसर में बांटे गए पोस्टर; 1984 के सैन्य अभियान और उसके राजनीतिक-सामाजिक प्रभावों को लेकर छिड़ी नई बहस

On the 42nd anniversary of Operation Blue Star, posters featuring Jarnail Singh Bhindranwale were reportedly distributed at Sri Akal Takht Sahib in Amritsar, Punjab. The occasion coincides with both the anniversary of the military operation conducted between June 1 and June 10, 1984, and the death anniversary of Bhindranwale, who was killed during the operation.

The development has once again brought attention to one of the most significant and debated chapters in modern Indian history. Operation Blue Star was launched by the Indian Army to remove armed militants from the Golden Temple complex, an event that continues to evoke strong emotions and differing perspectives across political, religious, and social spheres.

The distribution of posters has triggered discussions about historical memory, Sikh politics, national security, and the lasting impact of the events of 1984. As commemorations take place, the legacy of Operation Blue Star remains a subject of continued debate, reflection, and public discourse in Punjab and across India.

#OperationBlueStar #Amritsar #Punjab #IndianHistory #1984 AkalTakht NationalSecurity PunjabNews HistoricalEvents IndiaNews CurrentAffairs SikhHistory PublicDiscussion TrendingNews BreakingNews SouthAsia HistoryAndPolitics IndianArmy NewsUpdate ViralNews

tweets.title:
INDIA SLAMS PAKISTAN AT THE UN

India has strongly rebuked Pakistan at the United Nations, accusing it of misusing international forums to push “biased and false narratives” on Jammu & Kashmir.

India’s Permanent Representative to the UN, Parvathaneni Harish, stated:

“Being a member of the UN Security Council is a huge responsibility. It is not a forum for peddling biased and false narratives.”
tweets.content:
INDIA SLAMS PAKISTAN AT THE UN

India has strongly rebuked Pakistan at the United Nations, accusing it of misusing international forums to push “biased and false narratives” on Jammu & Kashmir.

India’s Permanent Representative to the UN, Parvathaneni Harish, stated:

“Being a member of the UN Security Council is a huge responsibility. It is not a forum for peddling biased and false narratives.”

tweets.title:
BREAKING: Oil India has discovered natural gas in the Sri Vijayapuram-3 exploratory well in the Andaman Sea, around 15 km off the Andaman Islands.

The well, drilled at a water depth of 355 m and beyond 1,900 m depth, showed continuous gas flow during initial testing.

The discovery under the Samudra Manthan Mission could strengthen India's offshore exploration efforts and long-term energy security.
tweets.content:
BREAKING: Oil India has discovered natural gas in the Sri Vijayapuram-3 exploratory well in the Andaman Sea, around 15 km off the Andaman Islands.

The discovery under the Samudra Manthan Mission could strengthen India's offshore exploration efforts and long-term energy security.

tweets.title:
We are witnessing one of the fastest tank development and induction programs in the world.

The only program getting close to Zorawar is the US M10 Booker's induction, which took around 8 years from development to induction.

Zorawar is at par with almost every capability of its Chinese and American counterparts, but Zorawar has some things that change the game for him against his Chinese counterpart.

Zorawar can swim, the ZTQ-15 can't !

Zorawar can fire and forget missiles, while ZTQ need to guide its missile through a laser designator, which means the Chinese ZTQ-15 might not be able to perform the shoot and scoot as effectively as Zorawar.

I know Zorawar faced some difficulty in Ladakh, I am hoping we can get these beasts inducted in 2027 breaking the record.

tweets.title:
BREAKING: U.S. forces have boarded the sanctioned supertanker Davina in the Indian Ocean as part of maritime enforcement operations targeting Iranian oil trade.

The vessel, capable of carrying up to 2 million barrels of crude, was sanctioned in 2024 for allegedly transporting Iranian oil. It was last tracked south of Sri Lanka and appeared to be heavily laden with cargo.

The interdiction comes amid escalating tensions over Iran's maritime activities and the Strait of Hormuz.
